description: Almost as if it were being controlled by something else, it never changes expressions, even in the middle of battle.

attacks.name: Teleport Blast
attacks.effect: You may switch Dark Alakazam with 1 of your Benched Pokémon (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon).

attacks.name: Mind Shock
attacks.effect: Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

name: Dark Alakazam

description: Freezes its prey with its stare. If you should encounter one, remember not to look into its eyes.

attacks.name: Stare
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.) If that Pokémon has a Pokémon Power, that power stops working until the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Poison Vapor
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Dark Arbok

description: An aggressive Pokémon that tackles anything it sees.

attacks.name: Hydrocannon
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each W Energy attached to Dark Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack. You can't add more than 40 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Rocket Tackle
attacks.effect: Dark Blastoise does 10 damage to itself. Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Dark Blastoise during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

name: Dark Blastoise

description: Seemingly possessed, it spews fire like a volcano, trying to burn all it sees.

attacks.name: Continuous Fireball
attacks.effect: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy cards attached to Dark Charizard. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads. Discard a number of Energy cards attached to Dark Charizard equal to the number of heads.

name: Dark Charizard

description: Sometimes called "The God of Destruction." Its wings are able to support it in spite of its massive weight.

abilities.name: Summon Minions
abilities.effect: When you play Dark Dragonite from your hand, search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Giant Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Dark Dragonite

description: Digs large sinkholes in the ground and tries to trap its enemies in them.

abilities.name: Sinkhole
abilities.effect: Whenever your opponent's Active Pokémon retreats, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, this power does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.) This power stops working while Dark Dugtrio is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Knock Down
attacks.effect: Your opponent flips a coin. If tails, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage; if heads, this attack does 20 damage.

name: Dark Dugtrio

description: Sneaks up silently on its prey and attacks it from behind, sometimes killing it with one bite.

abilities.name: Sneak Attack
abilities.effect: When you play Dark Golbat from your hand, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If you do, Dark Golbat does 10 damage to that Pokémon. Apply Weakness and Resistance.

attacks.name: Flitter
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

name: Dark Golbat

description: Normally found only in the deep ocean, it has recently been seen in shallow waters as well.

abilities.name: Final Beam
abilities.effect: When Dark Gyarados is Knocked Out by an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this power does 20 damage for each Water Energy attached to Dark Gyarados to the Pokémon that Knocked Out Dark Gyarados. Apply Weakness and Resistance. This power doesn't work if Dark Gyarados is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Dark Gyarados

description: Carries a pendulum with it always. Sometimes appears near less-traveled roads and schools at dusk.

attacks.name: Bench Manipulation
attacks.effect: Your opponent flips a number of coins equal to the number of Pokémon on his or her Bench. This attack does 20 damage times the number of tails. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

name: Dark Hypno

description: It is almost impossible to defend against a Machamp's four powerful arms.

attacks.name: Fling
attacks.effect: Your opponent shuffles his or her Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck. This attack can't be used if your opponent has no Benched Pokémon.

name: Dark Machamp

description: The number of incidents of hackers using Magnetons to damage computer systems is steadily increasing.

attacks.name: Sonicboom
attacks.effect: Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

attacks.name: Magnetic Lines
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has any basic Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them. If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and attach that Energy card to it.

name: Dark Magneton

description: It loves nothing better than to be left alone, and so sometimes ends up being manipulated by unscrupulous people.

abilities.name: Reel In
abilities.effect: When you play Dark Slowbro from your hand, choose up to 3 Basic Pokémon and/or Evolution cards from your discard pile and put them into your hand.

attacks.name: Fickle Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Dark Slowbro

description: Perhaps because of their strong-smelling pollen, other Pokémon do not approach areas where Vileplumes live.

abilities.name: Hay Fever
abilities.effect: No Trainer cards can be played. This power stops working while Dark Vileplume is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Petal Whirlwind
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. If you get 2 or more heads, Dark Vileplume is now Confused (after dealing damage).

name: Dark Vileplume

description: Researchers have observed these Pokémon reproducing in industrial waste sites.

attacks.name: Mass Explosion
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage times the total number of Koffings, Weezings, and Dark Weezings in play (Apply Weakness and Resistance.). Then, this attack does 20 damage to each Koffing, Weezing, and Dark Weezing (even your own). Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

attacks.name: Stun Gas
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned; if tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Dark Weezing

effect: Each player plays with his or her Prize cards face up for the rest of the game.
name: Here Comes Team Rocket!

effect: Look at your opponent's hand. If he or she has any Trainer cards, choose 1 of them. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck.
name: Rocket's Sneak Attack

effect: Attach Rainbow Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Rainbow Energy counts as every type of basic Energy but only provides 1 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card when not in play.) When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, it does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.)
name: Rainbow Energy

attacks.name: Stun Gas
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned; if tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Dark Weezing

description: Its tail is incredibly strong, able to lift up to 5 adult men.

attacks.name: Fireball
attacks.effect: Use this attack only if there are any Energy cards attached to Dark Charmeleon. Flip a coin. If heads, discard 1 of those Energy cards. If tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).

name: Dark Charmeleon

description: As is fitting for a legendary Pokémon, it has many strange powers, including the ability to make rain or snow fall.

abilities.name: Evolutionary Light
abilities.effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for an Evolution card. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Dark Dragonair is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Tail Strike
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

name: Dark Dragonair

description: Some researchers hypothesize that Electrode may actually be a form of energy instead of having an actual body.

attacks.name: Energy Bomb
attacks.effect: Take all Energy cards attached to Dark Electrode and attach them to your Benched Pokémon (in any way you choose). If you have no Benched Pokémon, discard all Energy cards attached to Dark Electrode.

name: Dark Electrode

description: A Pokémon with a hot temper that can't be controlled once it's angered. Capable of breathing flames hotter than 3000°.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Dark Flareon.

attacks.name: Playing with Fire
attacks.effect: Use this attack only if there are any Energy cards attached to Dark Flareon. Flip a coin. If heads, discard 1 of those Energy cards and this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage. If tails, this attack does 30 damage.

name: Dark Flareon

description: Its pollen has such a strong smell that it even confuses itself at times.

abilities.name: Pollen Stench
abilities.effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused; if tails, your Active Pokémon is now Confused. This power can't be used if Dark Gloom is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Dark Gloom

description: When Psyducks evolve, their headaches disappear and they find themselves in possession of formidable mental powers.

attacks.name: Third Eye
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Dark Golduck in order to draw up to 3 cards.

name: Dark Golduck

description: A dangerous Pokémon when angered. It likes to playfully shock people with the electricity it stores within its body.

attacks.name: Lightning Flash
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Thunder Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, Dark Jolteon does 10 damage to itself.

name: Dark Jolteon

description: Some people say that Kadabras are responsible for many of the mysterious events reported in the news.

abilities.name: Matter Exchange
abilities.effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a card from your hand in order to draw a card. This power can't be used if Dark Kadabra is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Mind Shock
attacks.effect: Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

name: Dark Kadabra

description: While normally peaceful and friendly, the number of incidents involving cruel Machokes is rising.

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with the Defending Pokémon. Do the damage to the new Defending Pokémon. This attack can't be used if your opponent has no Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Knock Back
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

name: Dark Machoke

description: Muks appear suddenly, spreading sludge everywhere. Whenever one appears in a city, panic ensues.

abilities.name: Sticky Goo
abilities.effect: As long as Dark Muk is your Active Pokémon, your opponent pays 2 more to retreat his or her Active Pokémon. This power stops working while Dark Muk is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Sludge Punch
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Dark Muk

description: Popular with women because of its beautiful fur. The leader of the Rockets keeps one as a pet.

attacks.name: Fascinate
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with the Defending Pokémon. This attack can't be used if your opponent has no Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Dark Persian

description: Although Primeapes have always been mean, lately they seem to be becoming even more vicious and frenzied.

abilities.name: Frenzy
abilities.effect: If Dark Primeape does any damage while it's Confused (even to itself), it does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Frenzied Attack
attacks.effect: Dark Primeape is now Confused (after doing damage).

name: Dark Primeape

description: A wild, uncontrollable Pokémon. When running, it looks like a comet streaking through the sky.

attacks.name: Flame Pillar
attacks.effect: You may discard 1 Energy card attached to Dark Rapidash when you use this attack. If you do and if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Dark Rapidash

description: A Pokémon capable of controlling water, so it is capable of creating giant whirlpools.

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

name: Dark Vaporeon

description: A Pokémon with a negative attitude, it hides in its shell whenever it's in a bad mood—which is often.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Mirror Shell
attacks.effect: If an attack does damage to Dark Wartortle during your opponent's next turn (even if Dark Wartortle is Knocked Out), Dark Wartortle attacks the Defending Pokémon for an equal amount of damage.

name: Dark Wartortle

description: Recent research has revealed the presence of dragon DNA in this species of Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rapid Evolution
attacks.effect: Search your deck for an Evolution card named Gyarados or Dark Gyarados and put it on Magikarp. (This counts as evolving Magikarp.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Magikarp

description: More and more people play the slots every day, trying to win a Porygon.

attacks.name: Conversion 1
attacks.effect: If Defending Pokémon has a Weakness, you may change it to a type of your choice other than Colorless.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Porygon

description: It teleports itself away from danger, making it difficult to capture.

attacks.name: Vanish
attacks.effect: Shuffle Abra into your deck. (Discard all cards attached to Abra.)

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Abra

description: Many people find it cute and try to hold it, only to be burned by its tail.

abilities.name: Gather Fire
abilities.effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may take 1 Fire Energy card attached to 1 of your other Pokémon and attach it to Charmander. This power can't be used if Charmander is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

name: Charmander

description: A Raticate's front teeth are incredibly powerful; it can destroy a two-story house with them in 5 minutes.

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. if tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Dark Raticate

description: It follows Onixes in their tunnels, looking for scraps of food they have left behind.

attacks.name: Dig Under
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after Applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

name: Diglett

description: The capture of the first Dratini inspired the search for other legendary Pokémon.

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Dratini

description: Not popular with girls because of its attitude. Can often be heard muttering to itself in a low voice.

abilities.name: Long Distance Hypnosis
abilities.effect: Distance Hypnosis - Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep; if tails, your Active Pokémon is now Asleep. The power can't be used if Drowzee is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Drowzee

description: A rare and unique Pokémon about which little is known, as few have been captured.

attacks.name: Sand-attack
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

name: Eevee

description: A carnivore that swallows its prey whole. Pidgeys and Spearows are its favorite food.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Ekans

description: It has recently been found that Grimers are formed in waste-processing plants, and can move into towns via sewers.

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Sticky Hands
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

name: Grimer

description: First discovered in a weapons factory, this Pokémon can now be found almost anywhere.

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Koffing

description: It's said that not even pro wrestlers can take down a Machop.
name: Machop

description: It is said that enough Magnemites and Magnetons gather in one place, they can create a powerful magnetic barrier.

attacks.name: Magnetism
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Magnemite, Magneton, and Dark Magneton on your Bench.

name: Magnemite

description: Normally friendly, it quickly becomes angry if it doesn't get what it wants.

attacks.name: Mischief
attacks.effect: Shuffle your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Anger
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

name: Mankey

description: A popular Pokémon that earns money for its owner with its "Pay Day" ability.

attacks.name: Coin Hurl
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

name: Meowth

description: It wanders around at night sowing its seeds to create more Oddishes.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Oddish

description: A gentle grass-eater, it will allow riders on its back. However, anyone attempting to do so must be careful not to get burned.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Ponyta in order to use this attack.

name: Ponyta

description: It is often seen holding its head in its paws, as if it were suffering from a headache.

attacks.name: Dizziness
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Psyduck but not used to pay for this attack. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

name: Psyduck

description: Loves to eat walnuts, chestnuts, cheese, and milk.

abilities.name: Trickery
abilities.effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch 1 of your Prizes with the top card of your deck. This power can't be used if Rattata is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

name: Rattata

description: Although it knows how to fish, no other intelligent behavior has been detected in it.

attacks.name: Afternoon Nap
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Energy card and attach it to Slowpoke. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Slowpoke

description: Cute Pokémon that charms people with its innocent eyes. Loves swimming all day long.
name: Squirtle

description: Usually seen only in power plants, it is sometimes seen in towns, apparently using the power lines to travel.
name: Voltorb

description: Lives in dark caves. Can occasionally be seen at night, when it flies in search of food.
name: Zubat

effect: Each player plays with his or her Prize cards face up for the rest of the game.
name: Here Comes Team Rocket!

effect: Look at your opponent's hand. If he or she has any Trainer cards, choose 1 of them. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck.
name: Rocket's Sneak Attack

effect: Search your deck for an Evolution card with Dark in its name. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
name: The Boss's Way

effect: Ask your opponent if he or she accepts your challenge. If your opponent declines (or if both Benches are full), draw 2 cards. If your opponent accepts, each of you searches your decks for any number of Basic Pokémon cards and puts them face down onto your Benches. (A player can't do this if his or her Bench is full.) When you both have finished, shuffle your decks and turn those cards face up.
name: Challenge!

effect: Flip a coin. If tails, do 10 damage to your Active Pokémon. If heads, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, your opponent does 10 damage to his or her Active Pokémon. If heads, you flip a coin. Keep doing this until a player gets tails.
name: Digger

effect: Discard a card from your hand in order to play this card. Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck, then draws 4 cards.
name: Imposter Oak's Revenge

effect: Choose up to 3 Basic Pokémon cards, Evolution cards, and/or basic Energy cards from your discard pile. Show them to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.
name: Nightly Garbage Run

effect: All Pokémon Powers stop working until the end of your opponent's next turn.
name: Goop Gas Attack

effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
name: Sleep!

effect: Attach Rainbow Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Rainbow Energy counts as every type of basic Energy but only provides 1 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card when not in play.) When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, it does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.)
name: Rainbow Energy

effect: If you play this card from your hand, the Pokémon you attach it to is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned. Full Heal Energy provides Colorless energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)
name: Full Heal Energy

effect: If you play this card from your hand, remove 1 damage counter from the Pokémon you attach it to, if it has any. Potion Energy provides Colorless energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)
name: Potion Energy

description: Stores up electricity in its body, then suddenly releases it to surprise and shock everyone.

attacks.name: Surprise Thunder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, flip another coin. If the second coin is heads, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If the second coin is tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Dark Raichu
